title: Muck Skip Derailment - Near Miss
description: Muck skip (2-cubic yard steel hopper car on rail gantry) derailed at Ring 290 gantry joint during normal haulage operation. Skip stayed upright - no tipping - 2 underground workers in adjacent work area were not in contact zone. Skip re-railed using chain falls and re-inspected. Root cause: worn rail joint flange allowed skip wheels to track off. All rail joints on gantry train inspected and 4 additional worn joints replaced. Formal near-miss investigation completed per OSHA 29 CFR 1926.
project_name: CTA Red Line Extension Utility Relocation Tunnel

title: Compressed Air Decompression Protocol Violation - Near Miss
description: During CO-001 TBM intervention at compressed air pressure (16.5 PSI), one worker was observed attempting to exit the man-lock before completing full decompression schedule per OSHA 29 CFR 1926 Subpart S Table P-1. Worker was stopped by second worker before exit. No injury. Worker counseled and re-trained on compressed air protocols. All compressed air workers required to re-certify on man-lock procedures. Tunnel physician notified per compressed air protocol.
project_name: CTA Red Line Extension Utility Relocation Tunnel

title: Hand Laceration - Segment Handling
description: Underground worker sustained 2-inch laceration on right palm during segment ring handling at erector. Worker was guiding 11-ton segment ring into position and hand contacted sharp edge of segment lifting socket. Cut glove and laceration treated by tunnel EMT on site. Sutures placed. Worker returned to modified duty following day. Revised: segment handling protocol requires use of certified lifting clamps only - no manual guidance permitted within 12 inches of erector jaws.
project_name: CTA Red Line Extension Utility Relocation Tunnel

title: Settlement Alert Level 1 - 10820 S Michigan Ave
description: Third-party settlement monitoring at 10820 S. Michigan Ave (8-story 1924 brick apartment building) triggered Alert Level 1 threshold of 0.5 inches total settlement at instrument SM-14 on January 14 2026. Immediate response initiated per monitoring plan: TBM advance temporarily slowed to 4 rings/day, compensation grouting initiated at 4 injection ports, monitoring frequency increased to hourly. No structural distress observed at building. Residents notified per Community Relations Plan. Settlement stabilized at 0.54 inches.
location: SM-14 Monitoring Point - 10820 S Michigan Ave
project_name: CTA Red Line Extension Utility Relocation Tunnel
